title: The new Honor Watch 6 packs a massive 980mAh battery in a slender 10.8mm body
description: The new Honor Watch 6 has a massive 980mAh battery, which can last up to 35 days in typical usage. The watch also has a lighter and slimmer body, a brighter display and improved health and sports tracking features. The Watch 6 body is made of recycled aluminum alloy with 316L stainless steel elements. It weighs 41g and measures 10.8mm thick – this is particularly impressive when compared against something like the Honor Watch 5 Ultra, which was 11g heavier (it weighs 52g), a bit thicker (11.4mm) and has only a 480mAh battery capacity. We say “only”, but 500mAh or so is actually pretty...

title: Honor X80 Pro Max will sport a 10,000-nit display
description: Honor is set to unveil the X80 Pro Max in China on June 22. Ahead of the launch, the company has confirmed that the phone will feature its largest battery to date and has already revealed its design and color options. It has now shared details about the handset's display. The Honor X80 Pro Max will feature an industry-first display capable of reaching 10,000 nits of peak brightness. Alongside its massive 11,000mAh battery, the display will be one of the phone's standout features. Honor also revealed that the panel will offer a 120Hz refresh rate, 1.5K resolution, and ultra-slim...

title: vivo starts teasing the global Y500, reveals its battery capacity and screen resolution
description: vivo launched the Y500 in China last September, and now a different Y500 family is ready to become available in other markets, comprising three members. One of those is the Y500 that vivo has just started teasing in Nepal. The phone looks nothing like the Chinese Y500. It has been teased to feature an AMOLED screen with "1.5K" resolution and an 8,100 mAh battery. There's also what vivo describes as a "trendy camera design" on board, as well as an "ultra fast & powerful" SoC, that unfortunately hasn't been officially revealed yet. vivo Y500 teasers However, a recent Google...

title: Google Home Speaker with Gemini finally has an official release date, pre-orders start
description: Back in August of last year, during the introduction of the Pixel 10 family, Google also teased an upcoming smart speaker. Then, it announced it as the Google Home Speaker in October. Now it's finally becoming available, ten months after the first teaser and eight months after the unveiling. The Google Home Speaker will be out on June 25 for $99.99, in four colors: Jade, Berry, Porcelain, and Hazel. Jade and Berry are "US-exclusive colors" for some reason. You can already pre-order one from the Google Store. The Google Home Speaker is powered by an unspecified quad-core CPU using...

title: OnePlus N6 confirmed to come with an 8,000mAh battery
description: Ahead of the OnePlus N6's launch later this month, the company has begun revealing key specifications of the smartphone. OnePlus is claiming that the handset offers the biggest battery in phones priced under INR 25,000. In a post on X, OnePlus confirmed that the N6 will pack an 8,000mAh battery, which is claimed to deliver up to three days of battery life. The company also says the battery is rated to maintain its health for up to 7 years. OnePlus hasn't shared any additional specifications yet, though more details are expected to be revealed ahead of the launch. The teasers...

title: Samsung starts taking pre-orders for the Galaxy XR in the UK, multiple deals available
description: Samsung launched the Galaxy XR headset in October of 2025, but it's so far only been available in Korea and the US. Now it's finally making its way to the UK, where the company has started taking pre-orders for it. You can pick one up for £1,699 and shipping is set to commence on July 8. It's only offered in one color: Silver Shadow. The Galaxy XR Travel Case and Galaxy XR Controllers are each priced at £249. However, you can get 30% off either accessory if you buy it alongside the Galaxy XR until September 30. Additionally, you can save £100 on your Galaxy XR purchase if you pay...

title: Gurman: Apple AirPods with built-in camera may arrive in late 2027
description: Back in May, a report claimed that Apple had entered the final stages of development for its camera-equipped AirPods. Now, Bloomberg's Mark Gurman has shared a revised launch timeline for the earbuds, along with a few additional details. According to Gurman, citing people familiar with the matter, the AirPods with built-in cameras are now expected to debut in late 2027. The earbuds will reportedly launch alongside Apple's second-generation foldable iPhone and the special 20th anniversary iPhone. Apple AirPods Pro 3rd gen In the earlier report, Gurman noted that Apple could delay...

title: Nothing CEO Carl Pei wants to steal Apple's customers
description: The latest publicity stunt by Nothing involves its founder and CEO, Carl Pei, releasing a video on his Instagram profile. In the video, he addresses Apple, warning the Cupertino company that Nothing is going to steal its customers, "one bored iPhone user at a time". And that's it. Does Tim Cook even know that Nothing exists? Does his successor? Probably not. View this post on Instagram A post shared by Carl Pei (@getpeid) Smaller companies do sometimes pull such stunts, name dropping much bigger competitors, in order to have people watching create an unconscious...

title: Wear OS 7 is now available for eligible Pixel Watch
description: Alongside the stable Android 17 release, Google has also started rolling out Wear OS 7 to compatible Pixel Watch models. The update introduces several new features and system improvements, while Google also claims it delivers better battery life. The Wear OS 7 update is currently rolling out to the Pixel Watch 2, Pixel Watch 3, and the latest Pixel Watch 4. In an official blog post, Google detailed the new features and improvements included with Wear OS 7. For starters, the company claims the update can improve battery life by up to 10% compared to Wear OS 6. The update also...

title: WhatsApp continues testing "view once" text messages on iOS
description: WhatsApp has started testing "view once" messages on iOS. As the name implies, these will be messages that you send and can only be viewed once by the recipient. After they viewed them once, the messages will disappear. To enable the functionality you will just need to long-tap on the Send button, which will then present you with the option to "Send as view once". Engage that and you're done. WhatsApp initially started testing this feature on Android earlier this month, and now the same is happening on iOS. The view once option is already available for photos, videos, and voice...

title: Sony unveils the Lytia 910, its first LOFIC sensor, and it boasts 100dB dynamic range
description: Sony just announced its first LOFIC image sensor, the Lytia 910. “LOFIC” stands for “Lateral Overflow Integration Capacitor”. In other words, it solves the issue of photodiodes becoming saturated by too much light by placing a capacitor next to each diode – this significantly increases the full well capacity of each pixel. According to Sony, the Lytia 910 will have 100dB of dynamic range – with a single exposure! For comparison, a sensor like the Lytia 901 needs multi-frame exposure to get to 100dB. The Sony Lytia 910 supports Triple Conversion Gain (TCG) HDR. This means that each pixel...

title: Motorola Razr 70 Ultra/Razr Ultra 2026 in for review
description: We finally got our hands on the Motorola Razr 70 Ultra (aka Razr Ultra 2026 in North America), and it's looking quite familiar. Here it is next to its predecessor - physically, aside from the different back panel on these specific two models, there's not much of a difference. You've got mostly the same displays - 7.0-inch 165Hz on the inside, 4.0-inch cover screen; the same SoC - a now-aging Snapdragon 8 Elite; even the camera system is unchanged. The screen on the new Razr Ultra has a few more nits at the high end, and the battery has grown to 5,000mAh (up from...

title: Snazzy Labs answers the question: is the Trump Mobile T1 Phone spying on you?
description: Quinn Nelson of Snazzy Labs is one of the first people to receive the Trump Mobile T1 Phone – and one of the funniest to review it. The video below is quite long, but it goes into great detail in examining the hardware and software of the phone. That said, the video actually starts with a 2-minute explanation of how email spam works because the Trump Mobile team had great difficulties in sending emails that don’t immediately get flagged by Gmail. And if you’re wondering whether you should get one, then we’ll just point out that the next 4 minutes of the video deal with all the difficulties...

title: OnePlus defies RAM crisis, offers the 15R with more memory
description: There's been a lot of talk recently about RAM prices going through the roof because of AI data centers gobbling up the entire supply, yet OnePlus doesn't seem to have gotten the memo. The company has just started offering the 15R with more memory than before, in India only for now. [#InlinePriceWidget,14358,1#] The phone launched last year with 12GB of RAM, and you can still get it with that amount of memory with either 256GB or 512GB of storage. But now there's a third option too, which pairs 16GB of RAM with 512GB of storage for INR 61,999 (approximately $657 at the current exchange...

title: Xreal Aura glasses unveiled with Android XR and the new Snapdragon Reality Elite
description: Xreal unveiled its new Aura glasses – these are spatial computing glasses that run Android XR, giving them support for a wide range of applications out of the gate. The glasses feature an Optical See-Through (OST) display that covers 70° field of view. This virtual display is created by two Sony Micro-OLED displays with 1,920 x 1,200px resolution and 120Hz refresh rate. The displays are transparent, so you can see your surroundings clearly and people around you can see your eyes. However, there is also electrochromic dimming for when you want to concentrate on what’s “on...

title: Xiaomi Mix Fold 5's launch timeline hinted at by new certification
description: Xiaomi's upcoming Mix Fold 5 may have just been certified for sale in China with the model number 2608BPX34C. This is interesting because the Mix Fold 5 has reached this step before any of the Xiaomi 18 family members. That could imply that the Mix Fold 5 won't launch alongside those devices in September, but will perhaps be introduced earlier. There's usually something happening in mid-August, and that sounds like a pretty good bet. Xiaomi Mix Fold 4 Unlike the Xiaomi 18 family, the Mix Fold 5 doesn't have to wait for Qualcomm to unveil the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 chipset, since...

title: Tata factory making iPhone backplates in India won't be shut down due to pollution
description: A factory supplying iPhone parts in India won't be shut down after all. The plant in Hosur, Tamil Nadu was under investigation by the Tamil Nadu Pollution Control Board (TNPCB) since December 2025, after farmers near the facility complained about factory wastewater contaminating their land and wells. The TNPCB warned of a possible forced shutdown of the facility. The factory is owned by Indian conglomerate Tata and manufactures iPhone backplates. Given Apple's recent drive to reduce its reliance on Chinese manufacturing, a quarter of iPhones sold worldwide are now made in India, with a...

title: vivo X Fold6's latest video teaser reveals its color options
description: vivo is set to unveil the X Fold6 on June 26 in China, and it has already revealed the smartphone's design and confirmed some features. Now, vivo has dropped a new video teaser, showing the X Fold6 in three different colors. Additionally, the video clip teases the vivo X Fold6's multi-tasking and productivity features, while reconfirming support for a telephoto converter. You can watch the video below. vivo hasn't detailed the X Fold6's specs sheet yet, but it previously confirmed the folding smartphone will come with a special-edition Dimensity 9500 SoC and run OriginOS 6 Fold. The...

title: Google expands Parental Controls and Family Link features with Android 17
description: Google introduced its new Android Parental Controls set of features alongside the Pixel 10 series last year, but they will be reaching more Android phones with Android 17. The new controls will give parents more control over their kids' phones and their digital wellbeing. Parental Controls are built into Android's Settings menu and offer a convenient way to track your kids' screen time, create a downtime schedule, apply Google Play app filters and limit the time spent on specific apps. Blocking apps altogether is also an option. For instance, parents can set up an automatic...

title: Samsung teams up with Spider-Man to promote its foldables
description: On July 31, Spider-Man: Brand New Day will have its premiere in cinemas all across the world. In the movie, Spider-Man will be using a Samsung Galaxy Z Flip, while Ned Leeds, another character, will try to locate him using a Galaxy Z Fold and a Galaxy Watch. To celebrate the paid product placement, Samsung has released the image you can see below, which could be showing the upcoming Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra. This will be the successor to the Galaxy Z Fold7 from last year, if the latest rumors are to be believed. It has the same aspect ratio, while the Galaxy Z Fold8 launching alongside the...
